danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character appearance in storyline and battles
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat to Earthrealm; invasion imminent
Shao Kahn's power amplified; danger escalated

danger_signals.visual_form: Crimson visual feedback on hits, Fatality animations
danger_signals.meaning: Mortal combat; lethal stakes; serious threat environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage design reflecting invasion (destroyed areas, altered environments)
danger_signals.meaning: Earthrealm under active assault; normal world corrupted

safety_signals.meaning: Agency and preparation; choosing champions for defense
Graduated challenge; player control over threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Mercy move prompt after round victory
safety_signals.meaning: Optional compassion reduces immediate lethality; player agency over life/death

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Liu Kang, Sonya Blade, Jax (playable human characters)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Various Earth-based stages (Subway, Bell Tower, Pit)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shao Kahn (boss), Sindel (resurrected bride), Smoke (robotic assassin)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earthrealm stages reflect functional civilization (subway, urban areas)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Warriors train and prepare; tournament-free narrative allows direct defensive action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corrupted/damaged environments; boss presence; Sindel as symbol of Outworld control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Warriors fight reactively against invasion force; story progresses toward confrontation with Shao Kahn

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Introduction of Animalities and Stage Fatalities (new destructive power displays)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat becomes more brutal; stakes visualized through graphic finishing moves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smoke (unlockable robotic character) via Ultimate Kombat Kode
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Secret warrior availability suggests hidden Outworld assets; unlocking requires meta-knowledge

environmental_storytelling.example: Subway, Bell Tower, and Pit environments communicate Earthrealm under siege; familiar urban locations transformed into battlegrounds

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Uppercuts break through ceilings to new stages
environmental_storytelling.example: Dynamic environment shifts indicate chaos of invasion; battles disrupt normal geography
Omission of Scorpion (franchise mascot) signals major narrative shift; character absences communicate changed world priorities
Shao Kahn and Sindel prominence in story/boss encounters establish them as dominant political/military force

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Playable character fighting through invasion; represents mortal champion resilience
Boss-tier opponent; requires progression through warrior gauntlet to confront; behavior symbolizes overwhelming threat

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Resurrected and allied with Shao Kahn
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Playable and boss character; resurrection demonstrates Shao Kahn's power; dual role shows Outworld infiltration
Unlockable only via Ultimate Kombat Kode; robotic nature suggests technological threat; secrecy signals covert Outworld operations

notes: MK3 shifts from tournament paradigm to invasion narrative, fundamentally changing how world-state is communicated. Danger signals cluster around Shao Kahn/Sindel presence and environmental corruption. Visual communication relies heavily on stage design, character roster presence/absence, and finishing move brutality to establish stakes. The Kombat Kode system and unlockable Smoke character add meta-layer of hidden threat communication. Gore and Fatalities serve dual function: mechanical reward and visual reinforcement of lethal world-state.
